content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN.

In one operation, Chef can remove **any one** element from the array.

Determine the **minimum** number of operations required to make all the elements **same**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN —the length of Array AAA.
Next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,A3,…,ANA_1, A_2, A_3, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​ - denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of operations required to make all the elements same.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 300000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer – the minimum number of deletions required to make all remaining elements equal.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: all elements already equal → 0 deletions.
Test case 2: value 2 appears 3 times, N=6 → 6-3=3 deletions.
Test case 3: value 2 appears 2 times, N=4 → 4-2=2 deletions.
Test case 4: every value is unique, max frequency =1, N=5 → 5-1=4 deletions.
First test: all five elements are 4 → 0 deletions.\nSecond test: value 2 appears 4 times, N=7 → 7-4=3 deletions.
All numbers are distinct, max frequency =1, so 9-1=8 deletions.

solution.approach: For each test case we count how many times each value appears. Let maxFreq be the largest frequency. Keeping all occurrences of the value with maxFreq already makes the array uniform, so we only need to delete the other N - maxFreq elements. The answer is therefore N - maxFreq. Counting can be done with a hash map (or an array when values are bounded).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) where K is the number of distinct values in a test case (≤ N).
